name: Chicken Pie with Cornmeal Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. About 1 1/4 hours before serving, discard bones and skin from chicken; cut meat into bite-sized pieces.
2. Cut potatoes into 1/2-inch chunks; set chicken and potatoes aside.
3. Preheat oven to 400°F. Slice mushrooms, carrots, and celery; chop onion.
4. In a 12-inch skillet over high heat, cook mushrooms, carrots, celery, and onion for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until vegetables soften slightly; stir in flour, salt, chicken bouillon, dried thyme, and pepper until vegetables are well coated. Add chicken, potatoes, and 3 1/3 cups milk to the mixture in the skillet.
5. Over high heat, bring to a boil for one minute.
6. Spoon the chicken mixture into a deep 3-quart casserole; bake, uncovered, for 10 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, prepare corn-muffin mix according to label instructions, using egg and remaining 2/3 cups milk.
8. After the chicken mixture has baked for 10 minutes, remove the casserole from the oven and spoon the cornmeal mixture over the top of the chicken mixture.
9. Bake for an additional 15 minutes or until the cornmeal crust is golden and the chicken mixture is hot and bubbly.
